source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A blue car of length \( 4.52 \text{ m} \) is moving north on a roadway that intersects another perpendicular roadway as shown in figure. The blue car has a constant acceleration of magnitude \( 2.10 \text{ m/s}^2 \) directed south. The time interval required for the nose of the blue car to move from the near (south) edge of the intersection to the north edge of the intersection is \( 3.10 \text{ s} \). How far is the nose of the blue car from the south edge of the intersection when it stops?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
the roadway/intersection geometry and at least one needed distance appear to be supplied by the figure rather than fully transcribed in text
multi_step: the problem combines motion over one interval ('from the south edge ... to the north edge' in 3.10 s) with a second target state ('when it stops')
it requires kinematics with multiple given quantities: car length, acceleration magnitude/direction, and traversal time
the setup likely needs figure-grounded geometry plus subsequent physics calculation, which is more than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the provided answer is a single numeric result with unit ('35.9m')
multiple-choice options are available, so the final result can be checked against choices
the expected solution is a deterministic kinematics calculation with checkable intermediate relations

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantics are recoverable
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scores are strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
although figure details are not fully transcribed in text, the required figure asset exists, so this is not a blocking gap
